active_alerts.description: Please see station personnel or use a station call box to request assistance.

Skipped outbound stops:
Tremont St opp St Mary's High School
Market St @ Tremont St

This is to accommodate track improvement work and the replacement of 14,000 railroad ties and is scheduled to complete mid-May, 2026.

Single-track operations will be in effect throughout this period so boarding areas may change.

Temporarily Suspended Inbound Trains:
•	126 (8:49am from Newburyport)
•	1132 (10:53am from Beverly)
•	46 (1:47pm from Rockport)
•	1150 (3:23 from Beverly)

Temporarily Suspended Outbound Trains:
•	125 (8:35am to Newburyport)
•	1129 (9:35am to Beverly)
•	39 (12:05pm to Rockport)
•	11:47am (2:05pm to Beverly)
Please see station personnel or use a station call box to request assistance.

Affected routes:
426
429
435
441
442
455

There are accessible mini-high platforms at the Western end of both the Track 1 and Track 2 sides. However, the existing pedestrian path between the two sides is long and includes steep slopes, especially on the Crest Road bridge over the tracks. Designs are underway to provide for a fully compliant pathway.

Affected direction: westbound

Affected stations:
Englewood Avenue

To exit, travel to the north end of the platform to the Orange Line-Red Line passageway and use Downtown Crossing Elevator 998 or 999 to access the Orange Line Oak Grove platform. To board the Red Line, travel down the Downtown Crossing Summer St concourse to the Orange Line Oak Grove platform and use Downtown Crossing Elevator 998 or 999 to access the Orange Line-Red Line passageway.

Starting Saturday, May 16, construction will be taking place at the Hingham Ferry Terminal. Buses will be bypassing Shipyard Drive during this work.

Skipped outbound stops:
Hingham Shipyard @ Ferry Terminal

Skipped inbound stops:
Hingham Shipyard @ Ferry Terminal
Scheduled to complete sometime in 2027, the Reconstruction of Foster Street includes widening and resurfacing with the addition of bicycle lanes.

Skipped outbound stops:
136 Georgetowne Pl

Skipped inbound stops:
136 Georgetowne Pl

Skipped inbound stops:
Hammond St @ Plympton St

Pedestrian Access:
There will be no access between the Hingham Ferry Terminal and DCR building. Pedestrian access to and from the ferry dock will be through and around the west side of the Hingham Ferry Terminal. Some sidewalk access at the Terminal may also be restricted during this work.

Shipyard Drive:
Shipyard Drive will be closed to traffic in front of the Hingham Ferry Terminal starting at 5 AM on Thursday, May 21. Traffic will be rerouted through the Hingham Parking lot. Drop-offs and pick-ups will be relocated to the parking lot. Traffic police and signage will be in place to direct traffic. This closure should last approximately one week.

Affected lines:
Foxboro Event Service
Providence/Stoughton Line

Affected routes:
60
65
Please use Back Bay Elevator 855 (Commuter Rail Track 1 & 3)

Skipped outbound stops:
Northern Ave @ Tide St

Skipped inbound stops:
Northern Ave @ Tide St
Please use nearby ramp to the platform.
To exit, exit the left side of the train onto the center platform and use Sullivan Elevator 840. To board, use Sullivan Elevator 840 to access the center platform.

Affected lines:
Fall River/New Bedford Line

Tappan Street will remain closed in the outbound direction only (toward Cleveland Circle) from June 13 through July 10.

Affected stations:
Tappan Street

Skipped outbound stops:
Oxford St @ Washington St
Oxford St @ Market St

To exit, use Aquarium Elevator 913 (Wonderland platform) or Aquarium Elevator 914 (Bowdoin platform) located at the opposite end of the platform to access the State St lobby, then use Aquarium Elevator 915 to access the street. To board, cross the Rose Kennedy Greenway, then continue west along State St to the Aquarium State St entrance and use Aquarium Elevator 915.

The escalator will remain out of service for the foreseeable future. This decision was made after a thorough inspection determined that corrosion on the escalator’s steel truss was so severe that it could not be repaired further and that a complete replacement of the escalator would be required. Replacing the escalator at this location requires a more extended unit meeting modern escalator safety requirements. This longer unit will require significant alteration to the station entrance to meet code requirements. The MBTA has currently begun design work to understand what station upgrades will be needed.

Due to these complexities, we expect the escalator will remain out of service for a significant time. We recognize the challenges this poses to our riders, and please know we are working to identify a solution as quickly as possible. In the meantime, Government Center Station, which is fully accessible and serves the Blue Line, is under a quarter-mile from Bowdoin Station.

To exit the Commuter Rail when traveling from North Station, take an Oak Grove-bound Orange Line train to Malden Center and use Malden Center Elevator 945. To exit Commuter Rail when traveling to North Station, exit the train at North Station, then transfer to an Oak Grove-bound Orange Line train to Malden Center and use Malden Center Elevator 945. To board the Commuter Rail, take a Forest Hills-bound Orange Line train to North Station, then transfer to a Haverhill Line train. Please notify station personnel or a train conductor if assistance is required.

To exit, use Oak Grove Elevator 745 located at the opposite end of the lobby to access the busway and parking lot. To board, travel to the intersection of Washington St and Winter St, then turn left onto Winter St. Proceed to Banks Pl, then turn left and continue to the busway/parking lot entrance and use Oak Grove Elevator 745 to access the lobby. See station personnel or use a station call box if assistance is required. Alternatively, customers may also use Bus Route 132 for transportation between the Oak Grove Washington St entrance and Malden Center.

To exit, exit the train at Savin Hill, then take an Ashmont-bound train to Fields Corner and use Fields Corner Elevator 958. To board, use Fields Corner Elevator 958 to access the Ashmont platform. Take an Ashmont-bound train to Shawmut, then transfer to an Alewife-bound train.
